---
title: "Ancestry-Aware PRS with genoformerR (v0.3)"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Ancestry-Aware PRS with genoformerR}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(eval = FALSE, echo = TRUE)
```

## What's new in v0.3

v0.3 replaces the separate `run_phasing` / `run_local_anc` flags with a single
`local_anc_method` argument and adds FLARE as the recommended default.

| Feature | v0.1 | v0.2 | v0.3 |
|---|---|---|---|
| Global ancestry FiLM | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Per-SNP local ancestry embedding | ❌ | ✅ (RFMix2) | ✅ |
| **FLARE** (no phasing required) | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ default |
| RFMix2 (legacy) | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| **Windowed PCA** (pure R, no reference) | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| Unified `local_anc_method` dispatcher | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |

## Choosing a local ancestry method

```
local_anc_method = "flare"         # recommended: fast, no phasing, biobank-scale
local_anc_method = "windowed_pca"  # no external tools — exploratory / no reference panel
local_anc_method = "rfmix2"        # legacy: best accuracy, but needs SHAPEIT4
local_anc_method = "none"          # global-only conditioning (v0.1 behaviour)
```

Decision guide:
- Admixed cohort + matched 1KGP3 reference → **flare**
- No reference panel / quick exploratory run → **windowed_pca**
- Phased data already in hand + need per-haplotype accuracy → **rfmix2**
- Continental-ancestry cohort or benchmark comparison → **none**

---

## Quick examples

### Global-only (no external tools)
```r
library(genoformerR)
gf_init()

results <- gf_run_pipeline(
  dosage_raw       = "data/plink/kg3_pgs_dosage.raw",
  pgs_file         = "PGS000018.txt.gz",
  panel_file       = "data/1kg/integrated_call_samples_v3.20130502.ALL.panel",
  phenotype        = pheno_vec,
  local_anc_method = "none"
)
```

### FLARE — recommended for admixed cohorts
```r
results <- gf_run_pipeline(
  dosage_raw       = "data/plink/kg3_pgs_dosage.raw",
  pgs_file         = "PGS000018.txt.gz",
  panel_file       = "data/1kg/integrated_call_samples_v3.20130502.ALL.panel",
  phenotype        = pheno_vec,
  local_anc_method = "flare",
  vcf_file         = "data/study.vcf.gz",      # unphased OK
  ref_vcf          = "data/1kg/kg3_ref.vcf.gz",
  sample_map       = "data/1kg/ref_panel.txt",
  gmap_dir         = "data/genetic_maps",
  flare_jar        = "tools/flare.jar",
  k_pops           = 5L
)
```

### Windowed PCA — zero external dependencies
```r
results <- gf_run_pipeline(
  dosage_raw       = "data/plink/kg3_pgs_dosage.raw",
  pgs_file         = "PGS000018.txt.gz",
  panel_file       = "data/1kg/integrated_call_samples_v3.20130502.ALL.panel",
  phenotype        = pheno_vec,
  local_anc_method = "windowed_pca",
  k_pops           = 5L  # number of pseudo-ancestry dimensions
)
```

---

## Using local ancestry functions directly

### FLARE step-by-step
```r
gf_init()

# Step 1: Run FLARE per chromosome (accepts unphased VCF)
flare_out <- gf_local_ancestry_flare(
  vcf_file   = "study.vcf.gz",
  ref_vcf    = "kg3_ref.vcf.gz",
  sample_map = "ref_panel.txt",
  gmap_dir   = "genetic_maps/",
  outdir     = "flare_output/",
  chroms     = 1:22,
  flare_jar  = "flare.jar",
  k_pops     = 5L
)

# Step 2: Parse and align to dosage matrix SNP order
local_anc_arr <- gf_parse_flare(
  flare_prefixes = flare_out,
  snp_ids        = colnames(dosage_mat),
  snp_positions  = snp_pos,
  snp_chroms     = snp_chr,
  sample_ids     = rownames(dosage_mat),
  global_anc     = anc$anc_probs   # fallback for non-admixed samples
)
dim(local_anc_arr)  # N × M × K
```

### Windowed PCA step-by-step
```r
# Pure R — no reference panel, no binaries
local_anc_arr <- gf_windowed_pca(
  dosage_mat  = dosage_mat,
  snp_chroms  = snp_chr,
  k_pops      = 5L,
  window_size = 500L,   # SNPs per window
  step_size   = 250L    # overlapping windows for smoother estimates
)

# Inspect: mean pseudo-ancestry per chromosome per labelled population
summary_tbl <- gf_local_anc_summary(local_anc_arr, snp_chr, pop_labels)
```

### Unified dispatcher
```r
# Switch methods without changing downstream code
local_anc_arr <- gf_local_ancestry(
  method        = "windowed_pca",   # or "flare" or "rfmix2"
  dosage_mat    = dosage_mat,
  snp_chroms    = snp_chr,
  k_pops        = 5L
)

# Plug directly into model training — same interface regardless of method
trained <- gf_train(
  model      = model,
  dosage_mat = dosage_mat,
  anc_probs  = anc$anc_probs,
  local_anc  = local_anc_arr,   # (N × M × K) from any method
  ...
)
```

---

## FLARE sample map format

Two-column headerless TSV. Reference panel samples only (not study samples).
Population labels define the K classes for local ancestry inference.

```
HG00096    EUR
HG00171    EUR
HG01880    AFR
NA19701    AMR
HG01595    SAS
```

For a Latino/Hispanic cohort, use K=3: `EUR`, `AFR`, `NAT`
(using 1KGP3 PEL/MXL as Native American proxy).

---

## Installing FLARE

FLARE requires Java ≥ 11:

```bash
# Check Java version
java -version

# Download FLARE jar (no compilation needed)
wget https://faculty.washington.edu/browning/flare.jar

# Verify
java -jar flare.jar 2>&1 | head -3
```

---

## Method comparison

| | FLARE | Windowed PCA | RFMix2 |
|---|---|---|---|
| Phasing required | No | No | Yes (SHAPEIT4) |
| Reference panel | Yes | No | Yes |
| External binary | Java jar | None | rfmix2 binary |
| Output type | Soft posteriors | Soft (softmax PCs) | Soft posteriors |
| Best for | Admixed, biobank | Exploratory, no ref | High-accuracy, phased |
| Typical runtime (22 chrs) | ~2 hrs | ~20 min (R) | ~8 hrs + phasing |

---

## References

- Cabreros & Storey (2023) — FLARE: Fast Local Ancestry REsolution
- Maples et al. (2013) *AJHG* — RFMix
- Delaneau et al. (2019) *Nature Communications* — SHAPEIT4
- Martin et al. (2019) *Nature Genetics* — PRS portability
- Ruan et al. (2022) *Nature Medicine* — multi-ancestry PRSice
